title: Tell Me How Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To share information or ideas with someone.
choices: communicate
emphasize
convince
rounds.answer: communicate
rounds.explanation: communicate: To share information or ideas with someone.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: To ask about something to get information.
choices: communicate
emphasize
inquire
rounds.answer: inquire
rounds.explanation: inquire: To ask about something to get information.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The outer edge or border of something.
choices: emphasize
fringe
communicate
rounds.answer: fringe
rounds.explanation: fringe: The outer edge or border of something.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A group of words that someone has said or written.
rounds.answer: quote

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To show or point out something.
rounds.answer: indicate

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: To come together as a group or team.
rounds.answer: unite

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.answer: Good writers communicate their ideas clearly.
I tried to convince my brother that reading is fun.
I will inquire about the homework for tomorrow.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

choices: To ask about something to get information.
To find your way through something.
To make something seem bigger or more important than it really is.
To show or point out something.
rounds.answer: To make something seem bigger or more important than it really is.
rounds.explanation: He exaggerated when he said the fish was a metre long!

choices: To find your way through something.
To pause before doing something because you are unsure.
To show or point out something.
To think something is true without proof.
rounds.answer: To think something is true without proof.
rounds.explanation: Do not assume — always check the facts.

choices: To ask about something to get information.
To come out or appear from somewhere.
To find your way through something.
To pause before doing something because you are unsure.
rounds.answer: To ask about something to get information.
rounds.explanation: I will inquire about the homework for tomorrow.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.target: Good writers communicate their ideas clearly.
I tried to convince my brother that reading is fun.
Explain one important unit idea, give a reason with because, and respond to two follow-up questions.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: A butterfly will emerge from its cocoon.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: She stood at the fringe of the group.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: The colours of the painting began to fade.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'navigate' mean in this unit?
choices: To find your way through something.
To share information or ideas with someone.
To make something seem important by saying it clearly or strongly.
rounds.answer: To find your way through something.
rounds.explanation: navigate: To find your way through something.

rounds.prompt: What does 'migrate' mean in this unit?
choices: To share information or ideas with someone.
To make something seem important by saying it clearly or strongly.
To move from one place to another.
rounds.answer: To move from one place to another.
rounds.explanation: migrate: To move from one place to another.

rounds.prompt: What does 'emphasize' mean in this unit?
choices: To make someone believe that something is true.
To make something seem important by saying it clearly or strongly.
To share information or ideas with someone.
rounds.answer: To make something seem important by saying it clearly or strongly.
rounds.explanation: emphasize: To make something seem important by saying it clearly or strongly.
